title: Database Data Migration Quiz
description: Test your fundamental knowledge about database migrations and their role in modern web applications.

questions.question: What is 'Database Migration' and why is it useful?
options: Moving data between different regions.
A systematic and version-controlled process of managing schema changes across different environments, ensuring consistency and allowing for rollbacks.
A type of database indexing.
A way to automatically compress files.

questions.question: What's the 'UP' part of a migration script?
options: To move the database to the cloud.
The commands to apply a new change (e.g., creating a new table).
To encrypt files using a secret key.
None of the above.

questions.question: What's the 'DOWN' part of a migration script?
options: To move the database back to a previous version.
The commands to undo a specific change (e.g., dropping a new table).
To automatically generate a backup of the database.
None of the above.

questions.question: Why is it important and generally better to use a migration tool like Prisma or Sequelize?
options: They make the database faster.
They ensure every developer and every server (including production) is running the exact same database schema.
To automatically generate a backup of the data.
To hide the application's IP address.

questions.question: What's a critical best practice when working with migrations?
options: Never edit a migration file after it has already been run and committed.
Create a new migration for every single SELECT query.
Always use a different database for every migration.
None of the above.
